message:
beads: file 17l4c (P2) + 5ob01 (P1) from fcp-audit static audit

Multi-pass static analysis of fcp-audit. Summary per pass:

Pass 1 (unwrap/expect/panic/debug_assert):
  Clean. All matches in crates/fcp-audit/src/lib.rs land after line
  1705 (#[cfg(test)] module boundary). No production panic surfaces.

Pass 2 (clock / timestamps):
  Receipt timestamps use u64 seconds supplied by the caller (not
  SystemTime::now()). `verify_chain_with_clock` at lib.rs:1466
  provides a +/-300s skew bound, correctly uses saturating_add for
  u64::MAX-at-adversary cases (lib.rs:3460 covers u64::MAX). Clock
  handling in isolation is fine; the problem is caller integration
  (see Pass 3 finding).

Pass 3 (signatures / verify):
  Concrete finding filed as flywheel_connectors-17l4c (P2).
  DecisionReceipt at lib.rs:743 has 13 fields, none of them a
  signature or issuer_kid. Compare to AuditEntry at lib.rs:177
  which DOES carry issuer_kid/signature. Receipts are the
  operator-facing "why allowed/denied" surface; a mid-flight
  mutation of decision/reason_code/explanation is undetectable
  downstream. Full fix plan (add sign/verify methods mirroring
  AuditEntry) in bead body.

Pass 4 (tamper chain / hash_prev):
  Hash chain itself is solid. verify_chain enforces prev pointer
  (lib.rs:1292), seq monotonicity (lib.rs:1281), timestamp
  non-decreasing (lib.rs:1305), genesis shape (lib.rs:1246), and
  recomputes canonical id to catch producer-supplied id forgery
  (lib.rs:1265). Clean.

Pass 3+4 follow-up — ORPHAN VERIFIER finding filed as
flywheel_connectors-5ob01 (P1). Workspace grep shows:
  - verify_chain_with_signers: zero production callers (only
    fcp-audit's own tests)
  - verify_chain_with_clock: zero production callers
  - fwc ships its OWN verify_chain at audit_chain/mod.rs:490 which
    checks hash linkage / seq / object_id / head — but grep for
    signature/verify_signature/issuer_kid/Ed25519 inside
    audit_chain/mod.rs returns zero matches.
So the live "fwc audit verify" path accepts chains composed
entirely of unsigned or forged-signature entries as long as the
hash linkage is internally consistent. Fix plan (wire
verify_chain_with_signers into fwc, or fold signature verification
into a single mandatory path) in bead body.

No trivial patches this turn — 17l4c needs a new sign/verify
surface on DecisionReceipt (API addition) and 5ob01 needs a wiring
decision across fwc's audit CLI. Both are code-owner calls, not
drive-by fixes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-5hmfa","title":"Probe Parented Task One","description":"Type: task","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:53:13.318073Z","updated_at":"2026-03-23T08:00:00.645155Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T08:00:00.645155Z","close_reason":"Probe task - not actionable.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-5ob01","title":"fcp-audit: verify_chain_with_signers/_with_clock are orphan — fwc ships its own unsigned verifier at audit_chain/mod.rs:490","description":"## Finding

Pass 3+4 of the fcp-audit multi-pass audit surfaced a trust-model hole: the only production audit-chain verifier in the workspace never checks signatures.

### Orphan library surface

`crates/fcp-audit/src/lib.rs` exposes three verification entry points:

  - `verify_chain` (line 1149) — hash-chain linkage + seq monotonicity only; explicitly documents "authenticity gap" at line 1132
  - `verify_chain_with_signers` (line 1432) — the one that verifies `issuer_kid`/`signature` per entry against a caller-supplied key resolver
  - `verify_chain_with_clock` (line 1466) — layers clock-skew bounds on top of `verify_chain`

Whole-workspace grep for production callers:

```
$ rg -n 'verify_chain_with_signers|verify_chain_with_clock' crates/ connectors/
crates/fcp-audit/src/lib.rs           # self-references + #[cfg(test)] call sites only
```

Zero callers outside `fcp-audit`'s own tests. `fwc`, `fcp-host`, `fcp-mesh`, and every connector never touch these APIs.

### Live production path bypasses signatures

`crates/fwc/src/audit_chain/mod.rs:490` defines its OWN `verify_chain` function (different signature, operates on `AuditEventRecord` rather than `fcp_audit::AuditEntry`). It is the one wired into `fwc audit verify` / the audit CLI. Grep for signature-adjacent terms inside that module:

```
$ rg -n 'signature|verify_signature|issuer_kid|Ed25519' crates/fwc/src/audit_chain/mod.rs
No matches found
```

So the live verifier:

  - ✓ validates hash linkage (prev_mismatch, fork_detected)
  - ✓ validates sequence monotonicity (seq_gap)
  - ✓ validates canonical object_id (object_id_mismatch)
  - ✓ validates head agreement (head_mismatch)
  - ✗ NEVER checks any signature
  - ✗ NEVER checks any clock-skew bound (so forged-future entries pass)

### Why this is P1

A chain composed entirely of **forged** entries whose `issuer_kid`/`signature` fields are either absent or gibberish will pass `fwc audit verify` cleanly as long as the hash linkage is internally consistent. That is exactly the threat model documented at fcp-audit/lib.rs:1132 ("A chain composed entirely of forged entries (any actor / any zone) signed by no one will pass `verify_chain` cleanly"). The mitigation documented at lib.rs:1136 ("use `verify_chain_with_signers` and supply a key resolver") is exactly what nothing does.

This silently violates the spec's **Tamper-Evident Audit** `PROVEN` claim in README.md ("Hash-linked audit chain with monotonic sequence numbers and quorum-signed checkpoints" — the "quorum-signed checkpoints" half is not enforced by the live verifier at read time).

### Fix plan

Three options, in order of increasing blast radius:

1. **Wire `verify_chain_with_signers` into `fwc audit verify`** (minimal): fwc's audit_chain/mod.rs already loads the key material required to resolve `issuer_kid` for CheckpointObjects elsewhere in the CLI; thread a `key_lookup` closure through and call into `fcp_audit::verify_chain_with_signers` after the local checks run. The existing local checks stay; signature verification is an additional layer, not a replacement.

2. **Deprecate fwc's local `verify_chain`** and route all CLI paths through `fcp_audit::verify_chain_with_signers` plus `verify_chain_with_clock`. Larger change but eliminates the duplicate verifier and the drift risk. Requires adapting `AuditEventRecord` → `fcp_audit::AuditEntry` at the boundary.

3. **Fold `verify_chain_with_signers` into `verify_chain`** so there is no opt-in path — every chain verification authenticates. Callers without keys opt out explicitly with `verify_chain_without_signatures` (rename to make the hazard loud). Biggest change; cleanest long-term.

### Verification after fix

  - Add a CLI integration test: craft a chain with valid hash linkage but a forged signature on entry N; current `fwc audit verify` passes it. After fix, it must return a `signature_invalid` issue.
  - Add a CLI integration test: craft a chain with `occurred_at` set one year in the future; current `fwc audit verify` passes it. After fix with `verify_chain_with_clock` wired in, it must flag `audit.timestamp_future`.

### Severity

P1 / HIGH. The audit chain is the system's tamper-evidence surface; its live verifier accepting unsigned chains defeats the tamper model. Filed during a static-analysis multi-pass audit of fcp-audit; related to bead 17l4c (DecisionReceipt no-integrity) but an independent finding in a different code path.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:37:50.487369Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:37:50.487369Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-5qp7o","title":"[MEDIUM] fcp-host verify_live_request: InstanceId::new() breaks instance-bound tokens + leaves non-bound tokens unchecked","description":"verify_live_request (bin/fcp-host.rs:1272-1276) constructs the CapabilityVerifier with a fresh random InstanceId per request:

    let verifier = CapabilityVerifier::new(
        capability_key.to_bytes(),
        request.zone_id.clone(),
        InstanceId::new(),  // fresh Uuid::new_v4() every call
    );

The verifier's instance-binding check (fcp-core/src/capability.rs:1505-1520) says:

    if let Some(inst_val) = claims.get(fcp2_claims::INSTANCE_ID) {
        let inst_str = inst_val.as_text()...?;
        if inst_str != self.instance_id.as_str() {
            return Err(FcpError::ZoneViolation { ... });
        }
    }
    // else: no instance check at all

So with a per-request random instance_id:
  (a) Any token that DOES declare `instance_id` is ALWAYS rejected — the caller's instance claim can never match a UUID the host just invented. Instance-bound tokens are effectively banned from the host.
  (b) Any token that DOES NOT declare `instance_id` passes without any instance check. Instance binding is silently opt-in for the token issuer and un-enforced by the host.

The host has no link from a running SubprocessConnector to its instance_id either — handshake uses `requested_instance_id: None` (bin/fcp-host.rs:220) and the connector chooses its own id, which the host never captures.

Net: instance binding is architecturally dead at the host gateway. Either:
  1. Honest: drop the instance check at the host entirely (pass an Option<InstanceId> to CapabilityVerifier and skip the check when None), OR
  2. Enforce: capture instance_id from handshake response into RegistryEntry and pass the real id so instance-bound tokens verify correctly against the connector they're addressed to.

Option 1 is the smaller change and matches current intent (host enforces zone+capability, not instance). Option 2 is the stronger security story (instance hand-off survives reissue/race).

Files: crates/fcp-host/src/bin/fcp-host.rs:1272-1276, crates/fcp-core/src/capability.rs:1505-1520.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T08:36:50.374308Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T08:46:36.967120Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T08:46:36.966571Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2790,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-5qp7o","author":"jemanuel","text":"Closed in commit 93299d9c. CapabilityVerifier.instance_id is now Option<InstanceId>; existing new() constructor wraps Some(id) so all 50+ call sites are unchanged. New without_instance_binding(key, zone) constructor skips the match check while preserving parser-level non-Text rejection. fcp-host::verify_live_request switched to without_instance_binding — instance-bound tokens now reach the connector where the check is meaningful (the connector knows its own InstanceId; the gateway does not). Three regression tests cover the accepts-bound-token, rejects-malformed-in-unbound-mode, and no-claim-no-check paths. Staged only the 5qp7o hunks in fcp-host.rs via filtered patch; parallel agent's in-flight t623k work preserved unstaged in the worktree.","created_at":"2026-04-20T08:45:41Z"},{"id":2791,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-5qp7o","author":"jemanuel","text":"Verified: fix landed in commit 93299d9c (br-5qp7o).

Core (crates/fcp-core/src/capability.rs):
  - CapabilityVerifier::instance_id switched to Option<InstanceId> (line 1392).
  - New constructor CapabilityVerifier::without_instance_binding (line 1421).
  - verify_claims_inner skips match when instance_id is None, but still rejects non-Text INSTANCE_ID claim as a parser-level violation (lines 1555-1574).
  - Three regression tests at lines 2294/2332/2371:
      * without_instance_binding_accepts_token_that_declares_instance_id
      * without_instance_binding_still_rejects_non_text_instance_id
      * without_instance_binding_ignores_tokens_without_instance_claim

Host (crates/fcp-host/src/bin/fcp-host.rs):
  - verify_live_request now constructs verifier via without_instance_binding (line 1350) with an explanatory comment citing this bead.
  - InstanceId::new() no longer imported in this file.

Result addresses both arms of the original finding:
  (a) Instance-bound tokens are no longer spuriously rejected by the gateway — the gateway honestly declines to enforce, deferring to the connector process.
  (b) A non-bound token is no longer silently accepted-without-check; the verifier explicitly documents that instance enforcement does not happen at this layer.

Remaining (intentional, separate beads): capturing the connector's real instance_id from handshake so the host CAN enforce (Option 2 in the original finding) is larger work and not required for this close — the LOW-severity doc/defense-in-depth piece continues in t623k.","created_at":"2026-04-20T08:46:33Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-5szl","title":"TEST: Agent Integration Unit Tests (Introspection, Tool Descriptors, Discovery)","description":"# TEST: Agent Integration Unit Tests

## Goal
Verify that FCP2 operations are discoverable and safe for AI agents.

## Test Categories

### 1. Introspection Schema Tests
- Introspection object schema validation (canonical CBOR)
- All required fields present (operations, schemas, risk levels)
- Golden vectors for introspection responses

### 2. Operation Discovery Tests
- Enumerate all connector operations
- Each operation has input/output schemas
- Risk tier correctly reported
- Rate limits correctly reported

### 3. MCP Tool Descriptor Tests
- Mapping to MCP-compatible tool descriptors
- Tool name, description, examples present
- Input/output schema translation
- Risk tier to MCP safety level mapping

### 4. E2E Discovery Tests
- Agent can enumerate tools from connector
- Agent can invoke safe operation end-to-end
- Agent can read recovery hints on failure

## Logging Requirements
- Structured JSON with operation_id, schema versions
- Discovery timing metrics
- Error details with recovery hints

## Acceptance Criteria
- Introspection is complete for agent operation
- No hardcoded connector knowledge required
- Tool descriptors match MCP conventions","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T08:50:32.192813258Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-28T20:59:34.312640984Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T20:59:34.312513066Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["agent","fcp2","mvp","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-5szl","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.30","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-5szl","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-5un1","title":"TEST: fcp-telemetry Unit Test Suite","description":"# TEST: fcp-telemetry Unit Test Suite


## Goal
Validate `fcp-telemetry` emits spec-required metrics/logging fields, preserves correlation, and never leaks secrets.

## Scope
Comprehensive unit tests for metrics, logging, and tracing library.

## Test Categories

### Structured Logging
- [ ] Log level filtering
- [ ] JSON output format
- [ ] Field injection (correlation IDs, zone, connector)
- [ ] Sensitive data redaction
- [ ] Log rotation handling

### Metrics Collection
- [ ] Counter increments
- [ ] Gauge values
- [ ] Histogram distributions
- [ ] Timing measurements
- [ ] Label/tag handling

### Distributed Tracing
- [ ] Span creation and nesting
- [ ] Context propagation
- [ ] Trace ID generation
- [ ] Span attributes
- [ ] Sampling decisions

### Export
- [ ] Prometheus format export
- [ ] OTLP format export
- [ ] Custom sink support
- [ ] Batch flushing

### Performance
- [ ] Low overhead in hot path
- [ ] Async export
- [ ] Buffer management

## Coverage Target: >90%

## Acceptance Criteria
- Runs deterministically in CI (no flake due to timing/network).
- Uses mocks only (no real external network).
- Emits structured JSON logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35` with stable `test_name` identifiers.

Covers: serde error cases, TraceContextError debug, metrics edge cases, Unicode handling, context propagation, TelemetryContext integration. Coverage target of >90% achieved.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp2","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-5un1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.28","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-5un1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-5un1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-krt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
